checklist_items.title: Verify robust error handling in TokenRouter.transferRemote
checklist_items.description: Check the implementation of the TokenRouter.transferRemote function and its internal calls for robust error handling mechanisms that prevent unexpected reverts and ensure availability.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The TokenRouter contract's transferRemote function or subsequent internal calls can revert unexpectedly.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust error handling in TokenRouter
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review report detailing error handling logic.

checklist_items.title: Assess reliability of TokenRouter dependencies
checklist_items.description: Review dependent contracts, such as Post-Dispatch Hooks, called by TokenRouter to ensure they are reliable, handle errors gracefully, and do not cause unexpected failures or reverts that impact TokenRouter availability.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Dependent contracts like Post-Dispatch Hooks are not fully reliable.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Ensure dependent contracts (like Hooks) are reliable
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review report or audit findings on dependent contracts.

checklist_items.title: Verify quoteDispatch robustness against reverts/failures.
checklist_items.description: Review the implementation of the quoteDispatch function in the Mailbox and configured Hooks to ensure it handles potential edge cases and inputs gracefully, minimizing the risk of unexpected reverts or failures that would prevent users from getting a fee quote.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Mailbox or Hook's quoteDispatch function is implemented such that it can revert or become unavailable.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust and available quoteDispatch functions; Provide alternative methods for estimating fees.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review report or audit findings.

checklist_items.title: Verify availability of alternative fee estimation methods.
checklist_items.description: Check if the protocol provides alternative methods or documentation for users to estimate message dispatch fees in scenarios where the quoteDispatch function might be temporarily unavailable or fail.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Mailbox or Hook's quoteDispatch function is implemented such that it can revert or become unavailable.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust and available quoteDispatch functions; Provide alternative methods for estimating fees.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Documentation link, client application code review, or test results showing alternative method usage.

checklist_items.title: Verify ISM implementation correctly validates message integrity
checklist_items.description: Review the smart contract code of the Interchain Security Module (ISM) used by the destination Mailbox to ensure it robustly verifies the integrity of the incoming message content (origin, sender, body, recipient, amount) against potential tampering by off-chain agents like the Relayer.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The ISM fails to detect the tampering.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Strengthen ISM verification
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code audit report or formal verification result for the ISM contract's verify function.

checklist_items.title: Assess implementation of redundant message verification mechanisms
checklist_items.description: Evaluate whether redundant verification mechanisms, such as using multiple independent Relayers or integrating Watcher services, are effectively implemented and configured to detect and prevent tampered messages from being processed by the destination Mailbox and recipient contracts like the TokenRouter.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A Relayer agent is compromised or malicious and can alter message content before delivery.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement redundant verification (e.g., multiple Relayers, Watchers)
checklist_items.evidence_required: System architecture documentation, configuration files for Relayers/Watchers, or test results demonstrating detection of tampered messages.

checklist_items.title: Establish monitoring and alerting for suspicious Relayer behavior
checklist_items.description: Implement monitoring systems to detect anomalous or suspicious behavior from Relayer agents, such as submitting messages with invalid formats, excessive failures, or patterns indicative of tampering attempts. Ensure alerts are triggered for investigation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A Relayer agent is compromised or malicious and can alter message content before delivery.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Monitor Relayer behavior.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Monitoring system configuration, alert logs, incident response procedures for Relayer anomalies.

checklist_items.title: Verify Multisig Requirement in ISM for TokenRouter
checklist_items.description: Verify that the ISM (Interchain Security Module) used by the TokenRouter requires a threshold of signatures from multiple validators to validate a message, preventing a single compromised validator from approving a fraudulent message.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A Validator agent is compromised or malicious and can sign fraudulent proofs.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require multiple validator signatures (Multisig ISM)
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review findings of the ISM contract, configuration details.

checklist_items.title: Verify Fraud Detection Mechanisms in ISM
checklist_items.description: Verify that the ISM or related smart contracts implement robust fraud detection mechanisms to identify and reject fraudulent proofs signed by validators (e.g., checking message hash against expected values, verifying origin chain state).
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The ISM relies on these proofs and fails to detect the fraud.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement fraud detection mechanisms
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review findings, test cases demonstrating fraud detection scenarios.

checklist_items.title: Verify Validator Agent Behavior Monitoring
checklist_items.description: Verify that monitoring systems are in place to detect suspicious behavior by Validator agents, such as signing conflicting proofs for the same message or signing proofs for invalid or malformed messages.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A Validator agent is compromised or malicious and can sign fraudulent proofs.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Monitor validator behavior
checklist_items.evidence_required: Documentation of monitoring setup, alert configurations, incident response procedures.

checklist_items.title: Verify Implementation and Configuration of Validator Slashing Mechanisms
checklist_items.description: Verify that slashing mechanisms are correctly implemented and configured within the protocol's smart contracts to economically penalize Validator agents who are found to have signed fraudulent proofs.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A Validator agent is compromised or malicious and can sign fraudulent proofs.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Slashing mechanisms.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review findings of slashing contract logic, configuration parameters, test cases for slashing conditions.

checklist_items.title: Verify on-chain message and proof authenticity checks in Mailbox contract
checklist_items.description: Review the Mailbox smart contract code to ensure it performs comprehensive verification of the interchain message and the accompanying proof submitted by the Relayer Agent. This includes verifying message origin, integrity, and cryptographic proof validity to prevent spoofing.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Mailbox contract does not fully verify the authenticity of the message or proof.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust on-chain verification of message authenticity and proofs.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review report detailing verification logic and test coverage.

checklist_items.title: Verify Mailbox performs cryptographic integrity checks on received messages/proofs.
checklist_items.description: Check the Mailbox smart contract code to ensure it validates the integrity of the submitted message and proof using cryptographic methods (e.g., signature verification, Merkle proof validation) before processing the message.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Mailbox's verification process is insufficient to detect tampering.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use ... cryptographic integrity checks.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review report highlighting relevant verification logic, test results demonstrating detection of tampered data.

checklist_items.title: Verify Support for Multiple Relayers
checklist_items.description: Check if the protocol's design and smart contract implementation allow for multiple independent Relayer agents to pick up and successfully deliver the same message, ensuring redundancy and reducing reliance on a single Relayer.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The protocol lacks effective mechanisms to ensure message delivery despite Relayer failures.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: support multiple Relayers
checklist_items.evidence_required: Protocol documentation, smart contract code review report.

checklist_items.title: Verify Permissionless Relaying
checklist_items.description: Check if the smart contract logic for message delivery is permissionless, allowing any agent to submit a valid message proof and complete the delivery process without requiring specific permissions or whitelisting.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The protocol lacks effective mechanisms to ensure message delivery despite Relayer failures.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: enable permissionless relaying
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract code review report.

checklist_items.title: Verify Relayer Incentive Mechanism Effectiveness
checklist_items.description: Check if the protocol's incentive mechanism for Relayer agents is designed and implemented to effectively encourage timely and reliable message delivery, mitigating the risk of Relayers failing or refusing service.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The protocol lacks effective mechanisms to ensure message delivery despite Relayer failures.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Incentivize Relayers
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract code review report, economic analysis of the incentive mechanism.

checklist_items.title: Verify Relayer Monitoring
checklist_items.description: Check if monitoring systems are implemented and configured to track the activity and health of Relayer agents and the status of message delivery, enabling timely detection of failures or delays.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The protocol lacks effective mechanisms to ensure message delivery despite Relayer failures.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: implement monitoring
checklist_items.evidence_required: Monitoring system configuration, alert policies, operational runbooks.

checklist_items.title: Verify Sufficient Relayer Capacity
checklist_items.description: Check if operational procedures and capacity planning are in place to ensure a sufficient number of active and healthy Relayer agents are running to handle the expected message volume and provide redundancy.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: There are insufficient active Relayer Agents.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: implement monitoring
checklist_items.evidence_required: Operational runbooks, capacity planning documents, monitoring dashboards showing Relayer count/health.

checklist_items.title: Verify sensitive message payload encryption.
checklist_items.description: Check if the protocol design and implementation ensure that sensitive data within interchain messages is encrypted before being handled by off-chain agents like the Relayer, preventing unauthorized disclosure if the agent is compromised.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Interchain Messages (Asset 100) contain sensitive information.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Encrypt sensitive message payloads or avoid including sensitive data in messages.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Design documentation review, code review of message handling/encryption logic.

checklist_items.title: Verify sensitive data exclusion from messages.
checklist_items.description: Check if the protocol design and application integration guidelines prevent the inclusion of sensitive information in interchain messages that are processed by off-chain agents like the Relayer.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Interchain Messages (Asset 100) contain sensitive information.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Encrypt sensitive message payloads or avoid including sensitive data in messages.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Design documentation review, application integration guidelines review.

checklist_items.title: Audit Mailbox contract message processing logic for privilege escalation vulnerabilities
checklist_items.description: Conduct a thorough security audit and code review of the Mailbox smart contract's message processing functions to identify and remediate potential vulnerabilities that could be exploited by a Relayer Agent to gain unauthorized privileges or modify state.
checklist_items.assumption_ref:
The Mailbox contract contains an exploitable vulnerability in its message processing function.
A Relayer Agent can craft a malicious message or proof to trigger the vulnerability.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Conduct rigorous smart contract audits and implement secure coding practices for the Mailbox.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract audit report detailing findings and remediation status.

checklist_items.title: Verify cryptographic proofs for Mailbox messages/checkpoints
checklist_items.description: Check that the Mailbox smart contract correctly implements cryptographic proofs (e.g., Merkle proofs) for messages and checkpoints to ensure their integrity before they are queried by the Validator Agent.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Messages or checkpoints in the Mailbox can be spoofed or tampered with before being queried by the Validator.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement cryptographic proofs for messages and checkpoints verified by the Validator.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review report confirming correct implementation of cryptographic proofs in the Mailbox contract.

checklist_items.title: Verify Validator Agent cryptographically signs verification outcomes.
checklist_items.description: Check that the Validator Agent's implementation includes cryptographic signing of the verification outcomes for messages and checkpoints, and that these signatures are published or made available for verification.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Validator Agent's verification actions are not recorded in a non-repudiable manner.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require Validator Agents to cryptographically sign and publish verification outcomes.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review of Validator Agent implementation, audit logs showing signed outcomes, on-chain data verification.

checklist_items.title: Minimize sensitive data in Interchain Messages
checklist_items.description: Verify that Interchain Messages (Asset 100) are designed to minimize or avoid containing sensitive information that could be exposed if processed by an untrusted off-chain agent.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Interchain Messages (Asset 100) contain sensitive information.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Minimize sensitive data in messages or encrypt sensitive portions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Documentation review, message format specification, or code review showing data structure design.

checklist_items.title: Secure Validator Agent operating environment
checklist_items.description: Verify that the operating environment where the Validator Agent (Actor 5) runs is adequately secured against data exfiltration to protect sensitive information processed from Interchain Messages (Asset 100).
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Validator Agent's operating environment is not fully secure against data exfiltration.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Secure the Validator Agent's operating environment.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Infrastructure security configuration review, access control lists, network segmentation details, audit logs.

checklist_items.title: Verify Validator Agent redundancy implementation.
checklist_items.description: Check if multiple Validator Agents are deployed and configured to ensure continued operation if one fails.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Validator Agent can be stopped or prevented from performing verification.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement redundancy... for Validator Agents.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Deployment configuration, monitoring dashboards showing multiple active agents.

checklist_items.title: Verify Validator Agent monitoring and alerting setup.
checklist_items.description: Check if monitoring is in place for Validator Agent health and performance, and if alerts are configured for failures or degraded performance.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Validator Agent can be stopped or prevented from performing verification.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement... monitoring, and alerting for Validator Agents.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Monitoring dashboard screenshots, alert configuration details.

checklist_items.title: Evaluate alternative verification methods or graceful degradation.
checklist_items.description: Assess if the system has alternative mechanisms to verify messages or can gracefully degrade functionality if Validator Agents are unavailable, reducing reliance on a single point of failure.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Message processing requires successful verification by the Validator Agent.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Consider alternative verification methods or graceful degradation.
checklist_items.evidence_required: System design documentation, protocol specifications, code review (if applicable).

checklist_items.title: Verify strong authentication for Scraper Agent database connection
checklist_items.description: Check if the Scraper Agent uses strong mutual authentication (e.g., TLS with client certificates) when connecting to the Postgres Database to prevent impersonation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The database connection does not require strong mutual authentication.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong authentication (e.g., TLS, client certificates) and granular access controls for database connections.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Configuration files or connection string details showing TLS and client certificate usage.

checklist_items.title: Verify granular access controls for Scraper Agent database user
checklist_items.description: Check if the database user used by the Scraper Agent has only the minimum necessary privileges (least privilege) on the Postgres Database and its tables to prevent unauthorized data access or modification.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The database access controls are not sufficiently granular.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong authentication (e.g., TLS, client certificates) and granular access controls for database connections.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Database user privilege listing or access control configuration.

checklist_items.title: Implement data validation and integrity checks for indexed data.
checklist_items.description: Verify that the Scraper Agent or the database enforces validation and integrity checks on data before it is written or updated in the Postgres Database. This prevents corrupted or falsified data from being stored.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The database allows modification of indexed data without sufficient validation.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement data validation and integrity checks on data written to the database.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review of the Scraper Agent's data writing logic or database schema/trigger configuration; test results demonstrating validation failures on invalid data.

checklist_items.title: Restrict database write permissions for the Scraper Agent.
checklist_items.description: Verify that the database user or role used by the Scraper Agent has only the minimum necessary write permissions required for its operation (e.g., INSERT, but not UPDATE/DELETE on historical data, or restricted columns).
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Scraper Agent has write permissions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Restrict write permissions for the Scraper Agent to only necessary operations.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Database user/role permission configuration screenshots or audit reports.

checklist_items.title: Verify critical database operations logging for Scraper Agent
checklist_items.description: Verify that all critical database operations performed by the Scraper Agent are logged.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Database logging is insufficient or can be tampered with.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust logging and monitoring of database interactions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Configuration details of database logging for the Scraper Agent user/role.

checklist_items.title: Check database log detail for Scraper Agent actions
checklist_items.description: Check if database logs include sufficient detail (e.g., user, timestamp, operation type, affected data) to uniquely identify and attribute actions performed by the Scraper Agent.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Database logging is insufficient or can be tampered with.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust logging and monitoring of database interactions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Sample log entries showing detailed information for Scraper Agent operations.

checklist_items.title: Confirm secure and tamper-evident database log storage
checklist_items.description: Confirm that database logs are stored in a secure, tamper-evident, or immutable location, preferably off-site from the database server.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Database logging is insufficient or can be tampered with.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Ensure logs are immutable or stored securely off-site.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Configuration and access controls for log storage location.

checklist_items.title: Ensure monitoring and alerting for database logs
checklist_items.description: Ensure monitoring and alerting are configured for suspicious or failed database operations recorded in the logs.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Database logging is insufficient or can be tampered with.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust logging and monitoring of database interactions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Configuration of monitoring and alerting rules for database logs.

checklist_items.title: Ensure database connections are encrypted
checklist_items.description: Verify that the connection between the Scraper Agent and the Postgres Database uses encryption (e.g., TLS/SSL) to protect data in transit.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The database connection is not encrypted.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Encrypt database connections (e.g., TLS).
checklist_items.evidence_required: Configuration settings showing TLS/SSL is enabled for database connections.

checklist_items.title: Ensure sensitive data is encrypted at rest in the database
checklist_items.description: Verify that sensitive indexed blockchain data stored in the Postgres Database is encrypted at rest to prevent unauthorized access to the stored files.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Data in the database is not encrypted at rest.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Encrypt sensitive data at rest in the database.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Database configuration or documentation confirming data-at-rest encryption is enabled.

checklist_items.title: Implement strict access controls for the database
checklist_items.description: Verify that access controls for the Postgres Database are configured to follow the principle of least privilege, restricting access only to authorized users and services (like the Scraper Agent) with necessary permissions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Implement strict access controls.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strict access controls.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Database user/role configuration and permission settings.

checklist_items.title: Verify database DoS protection measures
checklist_items.description: Check if the Postgres Database has implemented measures like connection limits, resource limits (CPU, memory), and rate limiting to prevent denial-of-service attacks.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The database is not protected against denial-of-service attacks (e.g., connection limits, resource limits).
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement rate limiting and resource controls on the database.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Configuration files, database settings screenshots, or audit reports showing DoS protection measures are enabled and configured appropriately.

checklist_items.title: Verify network resilience for database connection
checklist_items.description: Check if the network connection between the Scraper Agent and the Postgres Database is configured for resilience against disruption (e.g., redundant paths, failover mechanisms, monitoring).
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The network connection between the Scraper and the database is not resilient.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Ensure network resilience.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Network diagrams, configuration documentation, monitoring reports showing connection stability and failover capabilities.

checklist_items.title: Verify validation of database commands from Scraper Agent
checklist_items.description: Check if the Scraper Agent or an intermediary layer validates database commands before sending them to the Postgres Database to prevent malformed or malicious inputs that could cause denial of service.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Implement rate limiting and resource controls on the database. Ensure network resilience. Validate database commands.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Validate database commands.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review reports, application configuration settings, or test results demonstrating command validation.

checklist_items.title: Verify Scraper Agent database user adheres to least privilege
checklist_items.description: Check that the database user account used by the Scraper Agent only has the minimum necessary permissions to perform its function (indexing data), and does not have permissions for schema modification, user management, or other administrative tasks.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The database user for the Scraper Agent has more permissions than necessary (e.g., schema modification, user management).
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Apply the principle of least privilege to the Scraper Agent's database user.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Database user permission configuration details or script output verifying permissions.

checklist_items.title: Ensure Postgres Database software is patched and secured
checklist_items.description: Verify that the Postgres Database instance used by the Scraper Agent is running a supported version with the latest security patches applied and is configured securely according to best practices.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: There are vulnerabilities in the database or agent software.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Regularly patch and secure the database and agent software.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Database version information, patch history, and security configuration audit report.

checklist_items.title: Ensure Scraper Agent software is patched and secured
checklist_items.description: Verify that the Scraper Agent software is running the latest version with security patches applied and is configured securely.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: There are vulnerabilities in the database or agent software.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Regularly patch and secure the database and agent software.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Agent software version information and patch history.

checklist_items.title: Monitor TokenRouter contract availability
checklist_items.description: Implement monitoring systems to track the availability and responsiveness of the TokenRouter contract and its critical functions (like transferRemote) to detect and alert on potential denial of service conditions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The TokenRouter contract's transferRemote function or subsequent internal calls can revert unexpectedly.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Monitor contract availability.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Monitoring system configuration, alert policies, operational runbooks for availability incidents.

checklist_items.title: Verify secure communication channels for Relayer-Mailbox interaction
checklist_items.description: Check that the communication channel used by the Relayer Agent to submit messages and proofs to the destination Mailbox is secured using cryptographic protocols (e.g., HTTPS/TLS) to prevent tampering during transit.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The communication channel between the Relayer Agent and the Mailbox is not fully secure.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use secure communication channels and cryptographic integrity checks.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Network configuration documentation, security group settings, or code review of Relayer/Mailbox communication layer.
